prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a nearly black frame. A small “90’s Commercials” watermark is visible in the lower-right corner.

It then cuts to a bright, soft-focus domestic interior, apparently a bathroom or vanity area. White ceramic objects sit on a glossy counter near a window, including shallow bowls and lidded, rounded containers. The palette is pale and airy, with white fabric or towels in the background. A blurred hand or arm moves near the upper-right edge.

The next shot shows a warm tabletop or kitchen-like surface with produce or food items, including long orange vegetables resembling carrots. A large curved object in the foreground is out of focus, framing the scene. Plants and other household objects appear in the background.

The product is then shown clearly: a boxed item labeled “COUNTRY POTTERY” with “SOFT POTPOURRI” visible near the bottom of the package. In front of and beside the box are small white pottery-style containers with colored bands or bases in muted gray-blue, peach, and reddish tones. The box contains a white vase-like container.

The commercial continues with decorative home vignettes. One shot shows a bedside or tabletop arrangement with a framed photograph, a glass vase holding yellow flowers, and white pillows in the background. The look remains soft, bright, and domestic.

Another shot shows a mantel or shelf with carved trim. On it are pale decorative containers, including a tall rectangular vase or planter filled with greenery and white flowers. Other jars and household ornaments are visible in the softly lit background.

A later shot shows papers or a calendar-like page spread out on a bed or soft surface. Several numbered squares are visible, including “16,” “23,” and “30.” A pillow and loose papers appear around it, suggesting a home planning or bedroom setting, though no specific activity is fully shown.

The setting shifts to what appears to be a child’s room or nursery shelf. A pink stuffed rabbit, a large round ball, and a purple teddy bear sit near a lamp. Small toys or figurines are visible on the shelf.

Another domestic scene shows a countertop or table with fruit or small red items in a dish. A hand reaches in from the left, holding or moving a white pottery-like object near a tray. Other kitchen items and containers are visible in the warm, softly lit background.

The final frames present a product pack shot. Several small white pottery-style containers with colored lower bands are arranged in front of the boxed “COUNTRY POTTERY” product. The box is decorated with a floral or patterned design and again shows a white vase-like container inside. To the right is the visible “Johnson Wax” logo. Across the bottom, large white text reads: “Put Out The Pottery.” The image holds on this arrangement through the last frame.
